---
title: "Hapr Probit Interactive Demo"
author: "Maya"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: united
    highlight: tango
format: html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Introduction
This document provides an interactive demonstration of the `hapr_probit` function. We generate synthetic data, fit a model, and visualize predictions.

## Load Libraries and Setup
```{r, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(devtools)
library(listviewer)

devtools::load_all()
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
```

## Generate Fake Data
```{r}
n <- 1e4
var_v <- 1/3
var_epsilon <- 1/3
var_thetaw <- 1/3
true_improvement_ratio <- 1 / (1 - var_epsilon)

w <- data.frame(
  w1 = rnorm(n),
  w2 = factor(sample(c("A", "B", "C"), n, replace = TRUE))
)

v <- rnorm(n) * sqrt(var_v)
epsilon <- rnorm(n) * sqrt(var_epsilon)

gf <- w$w1 * sqrt(var_thetaw) + v
gc <- gf + epsilon
gc_normalized <- scale(gc) |> as.numeric()

y <- 0.42 * gf + rnorm(n) + 0.17 * w$w1 # True betas!
y_binary <- as.numeric(y > mean(y)) |> as.factor()
```

## Fit Hapr Probit Model
```{r}
fit <- hapr(y_binary, gc_normalized, w, model_type = "probit", improvement_ratio = 1.5)
```

### Model Coefficients
```{r}
print(fit$coefficients$beta)
```

## Simulating Data
```{r}
simulated_w <- simulate(fit, w = w) |> as_tibble()
simulated_w_gc <- simulate(fit, w = w, gc = gc_normalized) |> as_tibble()
```

## Predicting Outcomes
```{r}
predicted_w <- predict.hapr_fit(fit, newdata = simulated_w) |> as_tibble()
print(predicted_w)
```

## Visualizing Predictions
```{r}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3))
plot(predicted_w$y_hat_w, predicted_w$y_hat_w, main = "y_hat_w vs y_hat_w")
plot(predicted_w$y_hat_w, predicted_w$y_hat_gc_w, main = "y_hat_w vs y_hat_gc_w")
plot(predicted_w$y_hat_w, predicted_w$y_hat_gf_w, main = "y_hat_w vs y_hat_gf_w")
```

## Interactive Model Output
```{r}
listviewer::jsonedit(as.list(fit))
